# frozen_string_literal: true

module Langchain
  class Assistant
    module Messages
      class MistralAIMessage < Base
        # MistralAI uses the following roles:
        ROLES = [
          "system",
          "assistant",
          "user",
          "tool"
        ].freeze

        TOOL_ROLE = "tool"

        # Initialize a new MistralAI message
        #
        # @param role [String] The role of the message
        # @param content [String] The content of the message
        # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image
        # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
        # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
        def initialize(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil) # TODO: Implement image_file: reference (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/messages/object#messages/object-content)
          raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
          raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }

          @role = role
          # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
          @content = content.to_s
          # Make sure you're using the Pixtral model if you want to send image_url
          @image_url = image_url
          @tool_calls = tool_calls
          @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
        end

        # Check if the message came from an LLM
        #
        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
        def llm?
          assistant?
        end

        # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
        #
        # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash
        def to_hash
          if assistant?
            assistant_hash
          elsif system?
            system_hash
          elsif tool?
            tool_hash
          elsif user?
            user_hash
          end
        end

        # Check if the message came from an LLM
        #
        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
        def assistant?
          role == "assistant"
        end

        # Check if the message are system instructions
        #
        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message are system instructions
        def system?
          role == "system"
        end

        # Check if the message is a tool call
        #
        # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
        def tool?
          role == "tool"
        end

        # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
        # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
        def assistant_hash
          {
            role: "assistant",
            content: content,
            tool_calls: tool_calls,
            prefix: false
          }
        end

        # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
        # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "system"
        def system_hash
          {
            role: "system",
            content: build_content_array
          }
        end

        # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
        # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "tool"
        def tool_hash
          {
            role: "tool",
            content: content,
            tool_call_id: tool_call_id
          }
        end

        # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
        # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
        def user_hash
          {
            role: "user",
            content: build_content_array
          }
        end

        # Builds the content value for the message hash
        # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes, with keys :type and :text or :image_url.
        def build_content_array
          content_details = []

          if content && !content.empty?
            content_details << {
              type: "text",
              text: content
            }
          end

          if image_url
            content_details << {
              type: "image_url",
              image_url: image_url
            }
          end

          content_details
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

4	  #
5	  # Wrapper around Anthropic APIs.

18	      max_tokens: 256

22	    #
23	    # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
24	    # @param llm_options [Hash] Options to pass to the Anthropic client
25	    # @param default_options [Hash] Default options to use on every call to LLM, e.g.: { temperature:, completion_model:, chat_model:, max_tokens:, thinking: }
26	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Anthropic] Langchain::LLM::Anthropic instance
27	    def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})
28	      depends_on "anthropic"
29	
30	      @client = ::Anthropic::Client.new(api_key: api_key, **llm_options)
31	      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
32	      chat_parameters.update(
33	        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
34	        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
35	        max_tokens: {default: @defaults[:max_tokens]},
36	        metadata: {},
37	        system: {},
38	        thinking: {default: @defaults[:thinking]},
39	        request_options: {}
40	      )
41	      chat_parameters.ignore(:n, :user)
42	      chat_parameters.remap(stop: :stop_sequences)
43	    end
44	
45	    # Generate a completion for a given prompt
46	    #
47	    # @param prompt [String] Prompt to generate a completion for
48	    # @param model [String] The model to use
49	    # @param max_tokens_to_sample [Integer] The maximum number of tokens to sample
50	    # @param stop_sequences [Array<String>] The stop sequences to use
51	    # @param temperature [Float] The temperature to use
52	    # @param top_p [Float] The top p value to use
53	    # @param top_k [Integer] The top k value to use
54	    # @param metadata [Hash] The metadata to use
55	    # @param stream [Boolean] Whether to stream the response
56	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The completion
57	    def complete(
58	      prompt:,
59	      model: @defaults[:completion_model],
60	      max_tokens: @defaults[:max_tokens],
61	      stop_sequences: nil,
62	      temperature: @defaults[:temperature],
63	      top_p: nil,
64	      top_k: nil,
65	      metadata: nil,
66	      stream: nil
67	    )
68	      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
69	      raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if max_tokens.nil?
70	
71	      parameters = {
72	        model: model,
73	        prompt: prompt,
74	        max_tokens_to_sample: max_tokens,
75	        temperature: temperature
76	      }
77	      parameters[:stop_sequences] = stop_sequences if stop_sequences
78	      parameters[:top_p] = top_p if top_p
79	      parameters[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
80	      parameters[:metadata] = metadata if metadata
81	      parameters[:stream] = stream if stream
82	
83	      response = with_api_error_handling do
84	        client.complete(parameters: parameters)
85	      end
86	
87	      Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
88	    end
89	
90	    # Generate a chat completion for given messages
91	    #
92	    # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
93	    # @option params [Array<String>] :messages Input messages
94	    # @option params [String] :model The model that will complete your prompt
95	    # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens Maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping
96	    # @option params [Hash] :metadata Object describing metadata about the request
97	    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating
98	    # @option params [Boolean] :stream Whether to incrementally stream the response using server-sent events
99	    # @option params [String] :system System prompt
100	    # @option params [Float] :temperature Amount of randomness injected into the response
101	    # @option params [Array<String>] :tools Definitions of tools that the model may use
102	    # @option params [Hash] :thinking Enable extended thinking mode, e.g. { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 4000 }
103	    # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
104	    # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
105	    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The chat completion
106	    def chat(params = {}, &block)
107	      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
108	
109	      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
110	      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].empty?
111	      raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if parameters[:max_tokens].nil?
112	
113	      if block
114	        @response_chunks = []
115	        parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk|
116	          @response_chunks << chunk
117	          yield chunk
118	        end
119	      end
120	
121	      response = client.messages.create(parameters)
122	
123	      response = response_from_chunks if block
124	      reset_response_chunks
125	
126	      Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
127	    end
128	
129	    def with_api_error_handling
130	      response = yield
131	      return if response.empty?
132	
133	      raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "Anthropic API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")
134	
135	      response
136	    end
137	
138	    def response_from_chunks
139	      grouped_chunks = @response_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.except(nil)
140	
141	      usage_chunk = @response_chunks.find { |chunk| chunk["type"] == "message_delta" }
142	      usage = usage_chunk&.dig("usage")&.transform_keys(&:to_sym)
143	      stop_reason = usage_chunk&.dig("delta", "stop_reason")
144	
145	      content = grouped_chunks.map do |_index, chunks|
146	        text = chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "text") }.join
147	        if !text.nil? && !text.empty?
148	          {type: "text", text: text}
149	        else
150	          tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
151	        end
152	      end.flatten
153	
154	      first_chunk = @response_chunks.first
155	      {
156	        id: first_chunk&.dig("id") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "id"),
157	        model: first_chunk&.dig("model") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "model"),
158	        content: content,
159	        usage: usage,
160	        role: "assistant",
161	        stop_reason: stop_reason
162	      }
163	    end
164	
165	    def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
166	      return unless (first_block = chunks.find { |chunk| chunk.dig("content_block", "type") == "tool_use" })
167	
168	      chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.map do |index, chunks|
169	        input = chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }
170	          .map! { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }.join
171	        {
172	          id: first_block.dig("content_block", "id"),
173	          type: "tool_use",
174	          name: first_block.dig("content_block", "name"),
175	          input: input.empty? ? nil : JSON.parse(input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
176	        }
177	      end.compact
178	    end
179	
180	    private
181	
182	    def reset_response_chunks
183	      @response_chunks = []
184	    end
185	  end
186	end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Langchain::LLM
  #
  # Wrapper around Anthropic APIs.
  #
  # Gem requirements:
  #   gem "anthropic", "~> 1.10.0"
  #
  # Usage:
  #     llm = Langchain::LLM::Anthropic.new(api_key: ENV["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])
  #
  class Anthropic < Base
    DEFAULTS = {
      temperature: 0.0,
      completion_model: "claude-2.1",
      chat_model: "claude-sonnet-4-6",
      max_tokens: 256
    }.freeze

    # Initialize an Anthropic LLM instance
    #
    # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
    # @param llm_options [Hash] Options to pass to the Anthropic client
    # @param default_options [Hash] Default options to use on every call to LLM, e.g.: { temperature:, completion_model:, chat_model:, max_tokens:, thinking: }
    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Anthropic] Langchain::LLM::Anthropic instance
    def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})
      depends_on "anthropic"

      @client = ::Anthropic::Client.new(api_key: api_key, **llm_options)
      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
      chat_parameters.update(
        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
        max_tokens: {default: @defaults[:max_tokens]},
        metadata: {},
        system: {},
        thinking: {default: @defaults[:thinking]},
        request_options: {}
      )
      chat_parameters.ignore(:n, :user)
      chat_parameters.remap(stop: :stop_sequences)
    end

    # Generate a completion for a given prompt
    #
    # @param prompt [String] Prompt to generate a completion for
    # @param model [String] The model to use
    # @param max_tokens_to_sample [Integer] The maximum number of tokens to sample
    # @param stop_sequences [Array<String>] The stop sequences to use
    # @param temperature [Float] The temperature to use
    # @param top_p [Float] The top p value to use
    # @param top_k [Integer] The top k value to use
    # @param metadata [Hash] The metadata to use
    # @param stream [Boolean] Whether to stream the response
    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The completion
    def complete(
      prompt:,
      model: @defaults[:completion_model],
      max_tokens: @defaults[:max_tokens],
      stop_sequences: nil,
      temperature: @defaults[:temperature],
      top_p: nil,
      top_k: nil,
      metadata: nil,
      stream: nil
    )
      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
      raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if max_tokens.nil?

      parameters = {
        model: model,
        prompt: prompt,
        max_tokens_to_sample: max_tokens,
        temperature: temperature
      }
      parameters[:stop_sequences] = stop_sequences if stop_sequences
      parameters[:top_p] = top_p if top_p
      parameters[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
      parameters[:metadata] = metadata if metadata
      parameters[:stream] = stream if stream

      response = with_api_error_handling do
        client.complete(parameters: parameters)
      end

      Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
    end

    # Generate a chat completion for given messages
    #
    # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
    # @option params [Array<String>] :messages Input messages
    # @option params [String] :model The model that will complete your prompt
    # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens Maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping
    # @option params [Hash] :metadata Object describing metadata about the request
    # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating
    # @option params [Boolean] :stream Whether to incrementally stream the response using server-sent events
    # @option params [String] :system System prompt
    # @option params [Float] :temperature Amount of randomness injected into the response
    # @option params [Array<String>] :tools Definitions of tools that the model may use
    # @option params [Hash] :thinking Enable extended thinking mode, e.g. { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 4000 }
    # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
    # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
    # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The chat completion
    def chat(params = {}, &block)
      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)

      raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
      raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].empty?
      raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if parameters[:max_tokens].nil?

      if block
        @response_chunks = []
        parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk|
          @response_chunks << chunk
          yield chunk
        end
      end

      response = client.messages.create(parameters)

      response = response_from_chunks if block
      reset_response_chunks

      Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
    end

    def with_api_error_handling
      response = yield
      return if response.empty?

      raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "Anthropic API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")

      response
    end

    def response_from_chunks
      grouped_chunks = @response_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.except(nil)

      usage_chunk = @response_chunks.find { |chunk| chunk["type"] == "message_delta" }
      usage = usage_chunk&.dig("usage")&.transform_keys(&:to_sym)
      stop_reason = usage_chunk&.dig("delta", "stop_reason")

      content = grouped_chunks.map do |_index, chunks|
        text = chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "text") }.join
        if !text.nil? && !text.empty?
          {type: "text", text: text}
        else
          tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
        end
      end.flatten

      first_chunk = @response_chunks.first
      {
        id: first_chunk&.dig("id") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "id"),
        model: first_chunk&.dig("model") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "model"),
        content: content,
        usage: usage,
        role: "assistant",
        stop_reason: stop_reason
      }
    end

    def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
      return unless (first_block = chunks.find { |chunk| chunk.dig("content_block", "type") == "tool_use" })

      chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.map do |index, chunks|
        input = chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }
          .map! { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }.join
        {
          id: first_block.dig("content_block", "id"),
          type: "tool_use",
          name: first_block.dig("content_block", "name"),
          input: input.empty? ? nil : JSON.parse(input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
        }
      end.compact
    end

    private

    def reset_response_chunks
      @response_chunks = []
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Langchain::LLM
  # Gem requirements:
  #    gem "mistral-ai"
  #
  # Usage:
  #    llm = Langchain::LLM::MistralAI.new(api_key: ENV["MISTRAL_AI_API_KEY"])
  class MistralAI < Base
    DEFAULTS = {
      chat_model: "mistral-large-latest",
      embedding_model: "mistral-embed"
    }.freeze

    attr_reader :defaults

    def initialize(api_key:, default_options: {})
      depends_on "mistral-ai"

      @client = Mistral.new(
        credentials: {api_key: api_key},
        options: {server_sent_events: true}
      )

      @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
      chat_parameters.update(
        model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
        n: {default: @defaults[:n]},
        safe_prompt: {},
        temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
        response_format: {default: @defaults[:response_format]}
      )
      chat_parameters.remap(seed: :random_seed)
      chat_parameters.ignore(:n, :top_k)
    end

    def chat(params = {})
      parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)

      response = client.chat_completions(parameters)

      Langchain::LLM::Response::MistralAIResponse.new(response.to_h)
    end

    def embed(
      text:,
      model: defaults[:embedding_model],
      encoding_format: nil
    )
      params = {
        input: text,
        model: model
      }
      params[:encoding_format] = encoding_format if encoding_format

      response = client.embeddings(params)

      Langchain::LLM::Response::MistralAIResponse.new(response.to_h)
    end
  end
end

I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me write up the findings for each step, then the JSON summary.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** `langchainrb` — a Ruby gem (`langchain.gemspec`) providing a unified interface to many LLM providers, plus higher-level constructs (Assistant/agents, vectorsearch, prompts, tools, chunkers). Autoloaded by Zeitwerk via Rails::Engine (`lib/langchain/engine.rb:5-6` eager-loads `lib/`), so **new files need no manual `require`** — naming/namespacing alone registers them.

**Layout (the part that matters for a tool-calling provider):**
- `lib/langchain/assistant.rb` — the Assistant orchestrator (the agent loop).
- `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter.rb` — **provider selector** (`Adapter.build`).
- `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/*.rb` — per-provider assistant integrations (`base.rb` + 6 providers).
- `lib/langchain/assistant/messages/*.rb` — per-provider chat-message representations (`base.rb` + 6).
- `lib/langchain/llm/*.rb` — raw per-provider clients (`base.rb` + ~13 providers).
- `lib/langchain/llm/response/*.rb` — per-provider response wrappers (`base_response.rb` + ~13).
- `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/**` and `spec/lib/langchain/llm/**` — tests, mirroring the lib tree.

Four parallel "families" (adapter, message, response, client) each have a shared base and one impl per provider. Adding a provider means adding a member to each family plus a branch in the one selector.

## Step 2 — Trace: assistant → provider-specific request/response

Order of execution for one tool-calling turn:

1. `Assistant#initialize` builds the adapter once: `@llm_adapter = LLM::Adapter.build(llm)` — `lib/langchain/assistant.rb:57`.
2. `Adapter.build` selects the provider integration by `llm.is_a?(...)` — `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter.rb:8-24`.
3. `run` → `handle_state` → `process_latest_message` → `handle_user_or_tool_message` — `assistant.rb:143,249,261,302`.
4. `handle_user_or_tool_message` calls `chat_with_llm` — `assistant.rb:303,338`.
5. `chat_with_llm` formats the request via the adapter: `@llm_adapter.build_chat_params(...)` — `assistant.rb:341-347`, then calls the raw client `@llm.chat(**params, &@block)` — `assistant.rb:348`.
6. The client (e.g. `lib/langchain/llm/openai.rb:122`) performs the HTTP call and wraps the result: `Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse.new(response)` — `openai.rb:148`.
7. Back in the assistant, results are **read back** from that response: `response.role`, `response.chat_completion`, `response.tool_calls` → `add_message(...)` — `assistant.rb:305`; state transition keys off `response.tool_calls.any?` — `assistant.rb:312`.
8. When tools must run: `execute_tools` → `run_tool` → `@llm_adapter.extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)` parses the provider's tool-call shape — `assistant.rb:327,366`. Tool output role comes from `@llm_adapter.tool_role` — `assistant.rb:180,379`.

So the provider-specific seam is the adapter (request out + tool-call parse) and the response wrapper (results in), with the client in between.

## Step 3 — The adapter family and its contract

**Shared base:** `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/base.rb`. Contract (all `raise NotImplementedError`):
- `build_chat_params(messages:, instructions:, tools:, tool_choice:, parallel_tool_calls:)` — `base.rb:16` (format the request).
- `extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)` → `[tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]` — `base.rb:30` (read tool calls).

The adapter's `build_message` instantiates the message class; `tool_role`/`support_system_message?` read the message class's `TOOL_ROLE`/`ROLES`. The response wrapper is chosen inside the client (Step 5), not the adapter.

## Step 5 — Raw client family and its contract

**Shared base:** `lib/langchain/llm/base.rb` — `attr_accessor :client` (`:24`), `attr_reader :defaults` (`:27`); contract methods all `NotImplementedError`: `chat(...)` (`:50`), `complete(...)` (`:58`), `embed(...)` (`:67`), `summarize(...)` (`:76`); `chat_parameters` builds a `Parameters::Chat` for the unified-params mapping (`:83`); `default_dimensions` reads `DEFAULTS` (`:42`).

**Pattern (OpenAI `llm/openai.rb`):** `initialize` `depends_on` the gem + builds `@client` + sets `@defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(...)` + declares `chat_parameters.update/ignore/remap` (`:34-54`). `chat` runs `chat_parameters.to_params(params)`, calls `client.chat(...)`, and **wraps the raw hash**: `Response::OpenAIResponse.new(response)` (`:122-149`). Streaming is reassembled in `response_from_chunks` (`:185`).

**The wrap step per provider** (raw client result → Step-4 response object):
- OpenAI `llm/openai.rb:148` → `OpenAIResponse`
- Anthropic `llm/anthropic.rb:126` → `AnthropicResponse`
- MistralAI `llm/mistralai.rb:42` → `MistralAIResponse.new(response.to_h)`
- Ollama `llm/ollama.rb`, GoogleGemini `llm/google_gemini.rb`, GoogleVertexAI `llm/google_vertexai.rb`, AwsBedrock `llm/aws_bedrock.rb` — same shape.

So `client.chat` returns a `BaseResponse` subclass; the assistant only ever talks to that wrapper (Step 2.7).

## Step 6 — Every site that must change to add a tool-calling provider

For a hypothetical provider `NewProvider`:
1. **Client** — create `lib/langchain/llm/new_provider.rb` (`Langchain::LLM::NewProvider < Base`; `chat`/`embed`, wrap into its response). Model of: `lib/langchain/llm/openai.rb`.
2. **Response wrapper** — create `lib/langchain/llm/response/new_provider_response.rb` (`< BaseResponse`, implement at least `chat_completion`, `role`, `tool_calls`, token counts). Model: `lib/langchain/llm/response/openai_response.rb`.
3. **Assistant adapter** — create `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/new_provider.rb` (`< Adapters::Base`, all Step-3 methods). Model: `adapters/openai.rb`.
4. **Selection (the one branch)** — add `elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::NewProvider)` in `lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter.rb:8-24`.
5. **Message form** — create `lib/langchain/assistant/messages/new_provider_message.rb` (`< Messages::Base`, `ROLES`, `TOOL_ROLE`, `initialize`, `to_hash`, `llm?/tool?/system?`). Model: `messages/openai_message.rb`.
6. **Tool schema format** *(conditional)* — if the provider's tool JSON isn't OpenAI-shaped, add a `to_new_provider_format` to the function-schema builder (`lib/langchain/tool_definition.rb`); otherwise reuse `to_openai_format` like Mistral/Ollama do.
7. **Tests** — add: `spec/lib/langchain/llm/new_provider_spec.rb`; `spec/lib/langchain/llm/response/new_provider_response_spec.rb`; `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/new_provider_spec.rb`; `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/messages/new_provider_message_spec.rb`; a `context "when llm is NewProvider"` block in `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/assistant_spec.rb` (existing contexts at `:129,568,926,952,1134`); and the selection assertion in `spec/lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapter_spec.rb`.
